user_query:
I’m a patient, spoiler-tolerant film lover working on a tiny “Spoiler Hint Banner” idea for my personal movie notes. Please help me validate a simple rule and update my test plan so I can keep enjoying films while being mindful of others.

Inputs are in input/:
- input/movies.jsonl — my small dataset of movies with synopsis, review text, and user tags
- input/spoiler_keywords.txt — newline-separated spoiler keywords
- docs/TESTPLAN.md — a draft test plan with TODOs to replace

Rule to validate (acceptance threshold): show the Spoiler Hint Banner for a movie if the total count of keyword occurrences across synopsis + review is >= 2 (case-insensitive, simple substring matches; count every occurrence, even if in a negated phrase). Treat user_tags as containing a spoiler tag if the list includes the literal string "spoiler".

What I need:
1) Extract and scan data, then write output/spoiler_scan.csv with the following columns for each movie:
   - movie_id
   - title
   - keyword_hits (integer: total occurrences across synopsis and review)
   - matched_keywords (semicolon-separated unique keywords that appear at least once, ordered according to input/spoiler_keywords.txt)
   - flagged_by_threshold (true if keyword_hits >= 2, else false)
   - tags_has_spoiler (true if the movie’s user_tags includes "spoiler", else false)

2) Edit docs/TESTPLAN.md by replacing the TODO placeholders with:
   - A concrete Acceptance Criteria section that states the above threshold rule and that keywords are sourced from input/spoiler_keywords.txt and applied over both synopsis and review.
   - Exactly three concrete test cases (IDs TC-01, TC-02, TC-03) tied to the provided dataset, each listing: test case ID, movie_id, title, expected_keyword_hits, expected_flagged_by_threshold, and a short rationale noting the matched keywords. Use these three:
     • TC-01: Silent Shores — expect banner shown (true)
     • TC-02: Clockwork Garden — expect banner shown (true) though it lacks a spoiler tag
     • TC-03: Last Light — expect banner not shown (false)
   Ensure the expected_keyword_hits and expected_flagged_by_threshold are consistent with output/spoiler_scan.csv.

3) Compose a brief validation report at reports/validation_summary.md that includes:
   - The threshold used (>= 2)
   - Total movie count
   - Count of movies with flagged_by_threshold = true
   - Count of movies with tags_has_spoiler = true
   - A list of mismatches where flagged_by_threshold != tags_has_spoiler (bullet each with title, keyword_hits, matched_keywords, tags_has_spoiler)

Constraints:
- Do not change any input files except for editing docs/TESTPLAN.md as requested.
- Make all paths relative to the workspace, and ensure the two outputs exist exactly at output/spoiler_scan.csv and reports/validation_summary.md.
- Use case-insensitive substring matching for keywords; matched_keywords should reflect unique keywords that appeared at least once, in the same order as in input/spoiler_keywords.txt.

Thank you! The goal is to have a reproducible scan, an updated test plan with concrete cases, and a concise validation summary. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
